content:
5 min read
Just now

--

One of the first features you’ll eventually add to a web application is the ability to upload files. Whether users are uploading profile pictures, resumes, PDF reports, invoices, or images for an online store, Flask makes the process straightforward while still giving you complete control over how files are stored and processed.
Understanding file uploads is an important step in becoming a Flask developer because they combine HTML forms, HTTP requests, server-side validation, and file system operations into a single feature. Once you understand the basics, you’ll be able to build document management systems, image galleries, cloud storage integrations, and many other real-world applications.
In this article, we’ll build a simple Flask application that accepts uploaded files, saves them to the server, validates file types, handles errors gracefully, and serves uploaded files back to the user. Every concept is explained with working Python examples.
Before writing any code, install Flask if it is not already available.
pip install flaskCreate a project with the following structure.
project/
│
├── app.py
├── uploads/
└── templates/
└── upload.htmlThe uploads directory will hold every uploaded file. Flask will save files there after receiving them from the browser.
